approved_control.confirmed_meaning: Implement the lean pre-goal and reviewed replanning capability. This current full-chain orchestration is only the bootstrapping control path used because lean mode is not implemented yet; the target architecture must make lean pre-goal the default and keep full pre-goal as an exception.
required_answer_path: Preserve requirements.control.json and its semantic_base, required_outcomes, what_counts_as_done, work coverage, authority, and forbidden actions as the non-runtime-mutable anchor.
Introduce a generation-aware run model with run.control.json, current_generation, gen-N runtime/review metadata, and explicit current-generation validation.
Introduce lean pre-goal startup that can produce requirements.control.json, run.control.json, and gen-000/runtime.control.json without mandatory full design/goal/plan/review artifacts.
Introduce reviewed amendment events so runtime can report strategy mismatch and request derived-strategy changes without editing anchors or falsely completing.
Introduce orchestration rules that convert anchor-preserving amendment proposals into reviewed next generations and require human approval for anchor or authority changes.
Make compiler, guard, verifier, progress, evidence import, and final report generation-aware so only the current approved generation can satisfy completion.
Add historical regressions for api/v2 readiness substitution, checkpoint-only full-ceiling substitution, unresolved amendment success, stale generation, and automatic old-evidence carry-over.
what_is_not_enough_avoided: Keeping full design/goal/plan/review pre-goal as the default and merely documenting lean mode.
Adding only a post-hoc verifier while runtime continues executing a frozen strategy that cannot be revised.
Allowing runtime to mutate approved requirements, required outcomes, completion criteria, authority, or forbidden actions.
Letting old generation progress automatically satisfy a new generation.
Treating this bootstrapping full-chain orchestration as evidence that full pre-goal remains the future default.

required_steps.transition: Lean pre-goal default is represented in schemas, routing, and fixtures as requirements plus run.control plus gen-000/runtime without mandatory full design/goal/plan/review.
evidence: routing or orchestrating policy selects lean by default for exploratory work
tests showing lean startup validates without full-chain artifacts

required_steps.transition: Full pre-goal remains available but is selected only by explicit exception criteria.
evidence: routing/orchestrator policy lists full-mode exception triggers
tests showing high-risk or explicitly full workflows still require full artifacts

required_steps.transition: The official run model tracks current_generation and validates complete reviewed gen-N control facts.
evidence: guard rejection for stale or unreviewed current_generation

required_steps.transition: Runtime can emit structured amendment proposals when current strategy cannot produce approved outcomes, while approved anchors stay read-only.
evidence: progress event schema supports amendment events
using-control-json runtime protocol documents amendment triggers and anchor immutability

required_steps.transition: Orchestrator can review an anchor-preserving amendment into the next generation and stops for human approval when anchors would change.
Compiler, guard, and verifier operate on the current approved generation and reject stale, unresolved, or inconsistent generations.
evidence: verifier rejects unresolved amendment and non-current generation final claims

required_steps.transition: Evidence reuse across generations is explicit: imported evidence is declared and invalidated evidence cannot satisfy current generation completion.
evidence: verifier tests showing gen-000 evidence does not automatically satisfy gen-001

required_steps.transition: Historical semantic failures are encoded as regressions that fail or require amendment instead of allowing false completion.
